featTitle: Designing Genie: Pioneering UX Patterns for Agentic AI
featDescription: Built an agentic orchestration layer that transformed how teams reclaim creative focus. Learned n8n, AI development, and product thinking while automating routine design work to free the team for novel problems.
label: Read Case Study

entries.title: Future Positioning: Design in an Automated World
entries.description: Private planning document. Site positioning review, honest assessment of gaps, and what's worth building as AI automates the execution layer of design.

entries.title: Building Genie Changed Me
entries.description: The story of building Genie — from personal experiment to organizational platform — and what building it changed about how I work, what I'm capable of, and how others engage with me.

entries.title: When UX Becomes AX
entries.description: What designing for agentic systems actually looks like — mapping real workflows, defining agent behavior, setting guardrails — and why the skills designers already have are the ones that matter most.

entries.title: Designing Your Own Relevance
entries.description: How pressure, curiosity, and transferable skills created a career path no one handed me. On finding your niche, navigating identity shifts, and building relevance by staying grounded in what's true about how you work.

entries.title: Leading Design Innovation
entries.description: On what leading design as a strategic function actually looks like in practice: building infrastructure that changes what's possible downstream, automating the mechanical parts of delivery, and staying close enough to the work to make decisions that matter.

entries.title: The Ramstack
entries.description: A future-proof approach to front-end design isn't about specific tools—it's about the principles beneath them. On headless complexity, tech debt, and why interoperability should be the core of your design practice.

entries.title: The Future of Design
entries.description: Speculation on where design is heading. From siloed to integrated, static to dynamic, proprietary to open-source—the future of design is defined by tensions, not destinations.

entries.title: The Designer in the Age of AI
entries.description: Shifting from inspectors to system stewards. As tools automate comparison and regression, designers become orchestrators who define baselines, set standards, and protect coherence.
entries.alt: Article: The Designer in the Age of AI

entries.title: Design Tokens: A Shared Language That Outlasts the Tools
entries.description: Why a shared design language is the most resilient investment teams can make as tools, roles, and workflows keep evolving. In a time when everything else in tech feels like shifting sand, design tokens are the bedrock—tiny pieces of your design's DNA that become a shared language everyone can speak, ensuring consistency and adaptability even as the design tools around them evolve.
entries.alt: Article: Design Tokens - A Shared Language That Outlasts the Tools
entries.label: Read on Composable.com

entries.title: The Distance Between Request and Outcome Is Now Your Brand
entries.description: As agentic AI raises customer expectations for responsive, intelligent service, the gap between what companies promise and what they reliably deliver becomes their actual brand identity.
entries.alt: Article: The Distance Between Request and Outcome Is Now Your Brand
entries.label: Read on Orium

entries.title: Designing Genie: Pioneering Agentic AI for Delivery
entries.description: Designing agentic systems forces you to rethink how work gets done. Not in the abstract, but in the day-to-day reality of delivery: shifting requirements, scattered context, rising risk, and teams stretched thin. This article explores how we built Genie—an agentic orchestration layer that consolidates context, generates requirements, surfaces risks, and automates workflows—and the three mental model problems we solved in designing for agentic AI.
entries.alt: Article: Designing Genie - Pioneering Agentic AI for Delivery
entries.label: Read on Orium

entries.title: The Best QA Is the One That Doesn't Have to Happen
entries.description: Strong baselines. Early DQA. Less testing later. When quality is built into the baseline from the start, QA shifts from discovery to confirmation — and the best QA process is one that doesn't have to happen.
entries.alt: Article: The Best QA Is the One That Doesn't Have to Happen

entries.title: Designers as Final Checkpoint
entries.description: Maintaining authority through validation. When designers act as the final checkpoint before release, they protect the integrity of interaction, communication, accessibility, and system coherence.

entries.title: Manual vs. Automated Is the Wrong Debate
entries.description: The complementary role of human and machine intelligence. Automation is acceleration, not intelligence. The future of quality is machines for detection, humans for discernment.
entries.alt: Article: Manual vs. Automated Is the Wrong Debate

entries.title: Delivering Fewer, More Complete Things
entries.description: True velocity through completion, not volume. It's better to deliver fewer tickets that are truly done than more tickets that will be reopened. Rework is invisible drag.
entries.alt: Article: Delivering Fewer, More Complete Things

entries.title: The Guardrail Problem
entries.description: AI makes design fast. Dangerously fast. On why code friction is the discipline the process needs, and how to build constraints that let you run.

entries.title: Ego
entries.description: On the difference between the discomfort that makes you better and the kind that follows you home — and how to tell which one you're feeding.

entries.title: Make It Pop
entries.description: Finding joy in enterprise design when the craft has moved from pixels to systems, and why the human part of the work matters more as tools get better.

entries.title: Imposter Syndrome Never Fully Goes Away
entries.description: On showing up prepared without knowing the right answer, learning to sit with that discomfort, and being kind enough to yourself to build the support before you need it.
entries.alt: Article: Imposter Syndrome Never Fully Goes Away

entries.title: Design Learnings & Principles
entries.description: A living collection of wisdom from opinionated mentors, teachers, and personal design observations. From foundational principles to leadership lessons, this is a journal of continuous learning in design, code, and craft.

entries.title: Designing Genie
entries.description: How a personal automation experiment became a shared platform for the delivery team, and the three mental model problems that had to be solved to design for agentic AI.

entries.title: Token Pipeline for a Multi-Brand HMI Platform
entries.description: How I built a unified token architecture, an AI-guarded lint pipeline, and a one-command dev loop that kept Figma, code, and AI tooling in sync across four brands, two themes, and two modalities — for a multi-display vehicle Human-Machine Interface platform.
entries.alt: Case Study: Design Token Pipeline for a Multi-Brand HMI Platform

entries.title: The /design Agent
entries.description: An isolated Storybook environment powered by AI — one command to start. How I built a design layer on top of an agentic framework, and what I learned about operationalizing change in a service company where the best handoff is still a handoff.

entries.title: Fortune 100 Planning Tool
entries.description: A strategic planning tool for a Fortune 100 company — making complex organizational data across business units, budgets, and initiatives navigable without flattening it.

entries.title: OnLogic Product Configurator
entries.description: OnLogic's customers are IT professionals configuring industrial PCs with specific hardware requirements — where a wrong choice has real consequences. Migrated from a legacy platform to composable commerce, with a configuration flow built around real dependencies.

entries.title: Kum & Go Mobile Loyalty App
entries.description: Founded in 1959, Kum & Go redefines convenience with customer-centric offerings. Partnering with Orium, they launched a new digital experience featuring enhanced promotions, loyalty programs, and improved merchandising.

images.caption1: K&G Loyalty Platform - Mobile App Hero

entries.title: Softchoice Website Replatform
entries.description: When a brand launches a new identity, the website needs to reflect it—without breaking what already works. Redesigned Softchoice's website to reflect their new brand identity, working within their existing CMS structure. Enhanced modules, introduced motion design, and collaborated closely with development to ensure design fidelity.

entries.title: Design Token Starter
entries.description: A layered token architecture for personal projects and design systems — multi-brand, theme-aware, and Figma Token Studio ready. Two files to import, zero classes required.
entries.alt: Design Token Starter — multi-brand token architecture

entries.title: Counter Fill: Colouring the Holes in Your Type
entries.description: A script that fills the enclosed counter spaces inside letterforms — the holes in o, e, a, g — with any colour or gradient. Generative, font-agnostic, and built on real DOM text so it stays accessible.

entries.title: Design Guard
entries.description: A lightweight design guard that scans staged files for hardcoded lengths (px/rem/%/etc.) and colors, nudging code toward design tokens.

entries.title: Smart Quotes Fixer
entries.description: A safe script that converts straight quotes to smart quotes in rendered content without touching code, bindings, or attribute delimiters.

entries.title: Scroll-Driven Audio
entries.description: The page as an instrument. Scroll to build a beat from scratch — layered drum synthesis triggered by scroll progress, with genre modes for trap, dancehall, soca, and hip-hop.
entries.label: Open Experiment

entries.title: Editorial Photo Filters
entries.description: CSS, SVG, and canvas photo filters built with zero dependencies. Duotone, cyanotype, infrared, halftone, chromatic aberration, and more.
entries.label: Open Experiment

entries.title: Counter Fill
entries.description: Live demo of counter-fill — filling the enclosed counter spaces inside letterforms with colour or gradients, built on real DOM text.
entries.label: Open Experiment

entries.title: Explorations
entries.description: UI experiments, design process, and work-in-progress from across projects.

entries.title: Case Study Template
entries.description: Format reference for portfolio case studies. Demonstrates metadata block, section structure, blockquote talking points, and captioned images.
